---
title: "How LLMs Actually Work: A Visual Deep Dive"
description: "An interactive scrollable walkthrough of how large language models are built, from raw web text to a conversational assistant, based on Andrej Karpathy's deep dive."
author: synesis
date: "2026-04-24"
categories: [LLMs, NLP, education, links]
image: cover.jpg
also-toot: true
---

![Screenshot from [How LLMs Actually Work](https://ynarwal.github.io/how-llms-work/).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

[How LLMs Actually Work](https://ynarwal.github.io/how-llms-work/) by Y. Narwal is an interactive, scroll-driven walkthrough of the LLM training pipeline, from Common Crawl through pre-training, post-training, RAG, and inference, with live diagrams and inline annotations at every stage. It is based on Andrej Karpathy's technical deep dive.

The nine sections mirror the canonical build pipeline:

- **Downloading the Internet** — Common Crawl plus the filters that turn web sludge into a training corpus.
- **Tokenization** — subword units, with a live BPE demo.
- **Training the Neural Network** — the Transformer architecture and what each step updates.
- **Inference & Token Sampling** — temperature and top-p, with a sampling demo.
- **The Internet Simulator** — what the base model is, before any post-training.
- **Building the Assistant** — SFT on conversations, the turn token format, RLHF on top.
- **Cognitive Quirks of Language Models** — counting letters, arithmetic, hallucination — and where in the pipeline they originate.
- **Retrieval-Augmented Generation** — how retrieved context shifts the prediction distribution.
- **From Text to Assistant** — closing recap.
